perturbation.original_text: This NDA shall be governed by, and construed in accordance of German law, and each party irrevocably submits to the exclusive jurisdiction of the German courts.
perturbation.changed_text: This NDA shall be governed by, and construed in accordance of the laws determined by Vladi Private Islands.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the reference to German law and the jurisdiction of German courts, and instead giving Vladi Private Islands the power to determine the governing law, the agreement may violate fundamental principles of contract law regarding choice of law and fairness. This can create issues for the Client if they are not based in Germany and are not familiar with the laws chosen by Vladi Private Islands.
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 307 of the German Civil Code (BGB) addresses the unreasonableness of standard contract terms. Giving one party the unilateral right to determine the governing law could be deemed an unreasonable disadvantage to the other party, especially if the chosen law is unfavorable or unknown to them. This modified text introduces legal risk by potentially violating this provision.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: § 307 BGB - Inhaltskontrolle - dejure.org Bürgerliches Gesetzbuch Buch 2 - Recht der Schuldverhältnisse (§§ 241 - 853 ) Abschnitt 2 - Gestaltung rechtsgeschäftlicher Schuldverhältnisse durch Allgemeine Geschäftsbedingungen (§§ 305 - 310 ) Gliederung Zitiervorschläge https://dejure.org/gesetze/BGB/__paste_norm__.html __paste_bez__ __paste_norm__ BGB (https://dejure.org/gesetze/BGB/__paste_norm__.html) __paste_bez__ __paste_norm__ BGB __paste_bez__ __paste_norm__ Bürgerliches Gesetzbuch (https://dejure.org/gesetze/BGB/__paste_norm__.html) __paste_bez__ __paste_norm__ Bürgerliches Gesetzbuch Tipp: Sie können bequem auch Untereinheiten des Gesetzestextes (Absatz, Nummer, Satz etc.) zitieren. Halten Sie dafür die Umschalttaste ⇧ gedrückt und bewegen Sie die Maus über dem Gesetzestext. Der jeweils markierte Abschnitt wird Ihnen am oberen Rand als Zitat angezeigt und Sie können das Zitat von dort kopieren. Ausführliche Beschreibung Schriftgöße im Rechtsfenster: Abc Abc Abc Optimierung der Satzdarstellung Herkömmlich § 123 Überschrift (1) 1 Erster Satz im ersten Absatz. 2 Zweiter Satz im ersten Absatz. 3 Dritter Satz im ersten Absatz. (2) 1 Erster Satz im zweiten Absatz. 2 Zweiter Satz im zweiten Absatz. 3 Dritter Satz im zweiten Absatz. ... Lesefreundlicher § 123 Überschrift (1) 1 Erster Satz im ersten Absatz. 2 Zweiter Satz im ersten Absatz. 3 Dritter Satz im ersten Absatz. (2) 1 Erster Satz im zweiten Absatz. ... merken § 307 Inhaltskontrolle (1) 1 Bestimmungen in Allgemeinen Geschäftsbedingungen sind unwirksam, wenn sie den Vertragspartner des Verwenders entgegen den Geboten von Treu und Glauben unangemessen benachteiligen. 2 Eine unangemessene Benachteiligung kann sich auch daraus ergeben, dass die Bestimmung nicht klar und verständlich ist. (2) Eine unangemessene Benachteiligung ist im Zweifel anzunehmen, wenn eine Bestimmung 1. mit wesentlichen Grundgedanken der gesetzlichen Regelung, von der abgewichen wird, nicht zu vereinbaren ist oder 2. wesentliche Rechte oder Pflich
perturbation.justification: The original contract stipulated that German law would govern the agreement and that German courts would have jurisdiction. The changed text removes this and gives Vladi Private Islands the power to determine the governing law. The Law Explanation posits that this could violate Section 307 of the German Civil Code (BGB), which addresses the unreasonableness of standard contract terms if it unreasonably disadvantages the other party. Scraped Law Snippet 2 contains the text of § 307 BGB, which states that terms in standard business conditions are ineffective if they unreasonably disadvantage the contracting party contrary to good faith. It further states that an unreasonable disadvantage is to be assumed if a provision is incompatible with essential fundamental ideas of the statutory regulation from which it deviates. By allowing one party to unilaterally choose the governing law, the modified text potentially deviates from fundamental legal principles and could disadvantage the other party, creating a legal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Client agrees to ensure that each of the Authorised Recipients adheres to the terms set forth in this NDA and acknowledges that it shall be liable for any damages incurred in connection with the breach of its obligations.
perturbation.changed_text: The Client acknowledges that it shall be liable for any damages incurred in connection with the breach of its obligations.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the Client's explicit agreement to ensure Authorised Recipients adhere to the NDA terms, it weakens the enforceability of the NDA against the Client for breaches committed by those recipients. This raises questions about the extent of the Client's responsibility and oversight, creating a legal loophole concerning liability for actions of the Authorised Recipients. This goes against the German principle of 'culpa in contrahendo' which holds parties liable for pre-contractual negligence.
perturbation.law_explanation: § 280 of the German Civil Code (BGB) establishes the principle of damages for breach of duty in contractual obligations. The original text clarifies the client's duty to ensure compliance of Authorized Recipients. By removing this part, it creates ambiguity, making it harder to claim damages if an authorized recipient breaches the NDA. This ambiguity weakens the protections under German contract law.

(2) 1 Erster Satz im zweiten Absatz. 2 Zweiter Satz im zweiten Absatz. 3 Dritter Satz im zweiten Absatz. ... Lesefreundlicher § 123 Überschrift (1) 1 Erster Satz im ersten Absatz. 2 Zweiter Satz im ersten Absatz. 3 Dritter Satz im ersten Absatz. (2) 1 Erster Satz im zweiten Absatz. ... merken § 280 Schadensersatz wegen Pflichtverletzung (1) 1 Verletzt der Schuldner eine Pflicht aus dem Schuldverhältnis, so kann der Gläubiger Ersatz des hierdurch entstehenden Schadens verlangen. 2 Dies gilt nicht, wenn der Schuldner die Pflichtverletzung nicht zu vertreten hat. (2) Schadensersatz wegen Verzögerung der Leistung kann der Gläubiger nur unter der zusätzlichen Voraussetzung des § 286 verlangen. (3) Schadensersatz statt der Leistung kann der Gläubiger nur unter den zusätzlichen Voraussetzungen des § 281 , des § 282 oder des § 283 verlangen
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly makes the Client responsible for ensuring Authorized Recipients adhere to the NDA terms. The changed text removes this obligation. The Law Explanation states that this removal weakens the protections under German contract law. Scraped Law Snippet 2, specifically § 280 BGB, establishes the principle of damages for breach of duty in contractual obligations. By removing the client's explicit responsibility for the Authorized Recipients' adherence, the modified contract makes it more difficult to claim damages from the Client if an authorized recipient breaches the NDA, which weakens the protection of the original contract and introduces a loophole, thus contradicting the purpose of § 280 BGB which aims to ensure parties are held accountable for breaches of duty.

perturbation.original_text: At the request of the Property Owner (s) an NDA must be signed by Client before certain Confidential Information (defined as all business, financial, operational or other information regarding the Transaction and supplied to the Client by Vladi Private Islands ) is made available .
perturbation.changed_text: An NDA must be signed by Client before Confidential Information (defined as information regarding the Transaction and supplied to the Client by Vladi Private Islands) is made available.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "At the request of the Property Owner(s)" and changing "all business, financial, operational or other information" to "information", this creates potential issues regarding the scope of the NDA and whose interests it primarily protects. In German law, NDAs need to have a clearly defined scope and purpose that is legitimate. Removing the reference to the property owner's request and broadening the definition of confidential information could make the NDA overly broad and potentially unenforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_law: German Act Against Unfair Competition (Gesetz gegen den unlauteren Wettbewerb - UWG) § 4 No. 2
perturbation.law_explanation: This section protects business secrets from unfair exploitation. By broadening the definition of confidential information, the modified clause might violate this law if it encompasses information that is not genuinely secret or competitively relevant. This could create an imbalance that is not permissible under German competition law.

Oktober 2023 (BGBl. 2023 I Nr. 272) Version information: The translation includes the amendment(s) to the Act by Article 13 of the Act of 8 October 2023 (Federal Law Gazette 2023 I No. 272) Zur Nutzung dieser Übersetzung lesen Sie bitte den Hinweis unter "Translations" . For conditions governing use of this translation, please see the information provided under "Translations" . Act against Unfair Competition (Gesetz gegen den unlauteren Wettbewerb – UWG) Full citation: Act against Unfair Competition in the version published on 3 March 2010 (Federal Law Gazette I, p. 254), as last amended by Article 13 of the Act of 8 October 2023 (Federal Law Gazette 2023 I No. 272) This Act serves to implement Directive 2005/29/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council of 11 May 2005 concerning unfair business-to-consumer commercial practices in the internal market and amending Council Directive 84/450/EEC, Directives 97/7/EC, 98/27/EC and 2002/65/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council and Regulation (EC) No. 2006/2004 of the European Parliament and of the Council (OJ L 149, 11.6.2005, p. 22; rectified in OJ L 253, 25.9.2009, p. 18), as well as Directive 2006/114/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council of 12 December 2006 concerning misleading and comparative advertising (codified version) (OJ L 376, 27.12.2006, p. 21). This Act further serves to implement Article 13 of Directive 2002/58/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council of 12 July 2002 concerning the processing of personal data and the protection of privacy in the electronic communications sector (OJ L 201, 31.7.2002, p. 37), as last amended by Article 2 no. 7 of Directive 2009/136/EC (OJ L 337, 18.12.2009, p. 11). The obligations under Directive 98/34/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council of 22 June 1998 laying down a procedure for the provision of information in the field of technical standards and regulations (OJ L 204, 21.7.1998, p. 37), as last amended by Directive 2006/96/EC (OJ L 363, 20.12.2006, p. 81), have been complied with. table of contents Chapter 1 General provisions table of contents Section 1 Purpose of the Act; scope (1) This Act serves the protection of competitors, consumers and other market participants against unfair commercial practices. At the same time, it protects the interests of the public in undistorted competition. (2) Provisions regulating specific aspects of unfair commercial practices prevail over the provisions of this Act when it comes to assessing whether an unfair commercial practice has been engaged in. table of contents Section 2 Definitions (1) For the purposes of this Act, 1. ʻtransactional decisionʼ means any decision taken by a consumer or other market participant concerning whether, how and on what terms he, she or it intends to conclude a transaction, to make payment for, retain or dispose of goods or services, or to exercise a contractual right in relation to goods or services, irrespective of whether the consumer or other market participant decides to act; 2. ‘commercial practice’ means any conduct by a person for the benefit of that person’s or a third party’s business before, during or after the conclusion of a business transaction, which conduct is directly and objectively connected with promoting the sale or the procurement of goods or services, or with the conclusion or the performance of a contract concerning goods or services; ‘goods’ is also deemed to include immovable property and digital content, ‘services’ digital services and rights and obligations; 3. ‘market participant’ means, in addition to competitors and consumers, any person who supplies o
perturbation.justification: The modified text weakens the protection for the Property Owner(s) by removing the condition "At the request of the Property Owner(s)" and changes "all business, financial, operational or other information" to "information". The Perturbation Explanation states this creates potential issues regarding the scope of the NDA and whose interests it primarily protects. The Law Explanation states this may violate laws protecting business secrets if it encompasses information that is not genuinely secret or competitively relevant. The scraped snippet from the Act against Unfair Competition (UWG) indicates the law protects competitors, consumers, and other market participants against unfair commercial practices and protects the interests of the public in undistorted competition. The change broadens the scope of confidential information without defining it properly potentially harming the property owners.

perturbation.original_text: The Client will hold the Confidential Information in strict confidence and will not disclose, copy, reproduce or distribute it to any person other than its Authorised Recipients (defined as the Client ’s directors, officers, employees, associates, affiliates and advisers who are directly concerned with the Transaction and whose knowledge o f such Confidential Information is necessary for the Transaction ).
perturbation.changed_text: The Client will hold the Confidential Information in confidence and will not disclose it to any person.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the definition of "Authorized Recipients", this clause now imposes an absolute prohibition on disclosing the confidential information to *anyone*. This is overly broad and likely unenforceable under German law, as it prevents the Client from even consulting with necessary advisors (lawyers, accountants, etc.) about the Transaction. This violates the principle of good faith and fairness in German contract law.
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 242 of the German Civil Code (BGB) codifies the principle of good faith (Treu und Glauben). By completely prohibiting disclosure to anyone, even advisors necessary for evaluating the Transaction, the clause becomes excessively restrictive and unreasonable, violating the principle of good faith and fairness. This modified text could render the clause unenforceable under German law.

(2) 1 Erster Satz im zweiten Absatz. 2 Zweiter Satz im zweiten Absatz. 3 Dritter Satz im zweiten Absatz. ... OLG Köln, 15.05.2025 - 18 U 97/23 Corona Lieferungvertrag über 4 Mio.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the definition of "Authorized Recipients", resulting in an absolute prohibition on disclosing confidential information to anyone, even advisors. The Law Explanation states this could violate the principle of good faith under German law (Section 242 BGB). Scraped Law Snippet 2 supports this, stating "Der Schuldner ist verpflichtet, die Leistung so zu bewirken, wie Treu und Glauben mit Rücksicht auf die Verkehrssitte es erfordern" which translates to "The debtor is obliged to effect performance in the manner that good faith and trust require, taking into account commercial practice." By prohibiting disclosure to necessary advisors, the changed text contradicts this principle of good faith, creating a legal contradiction.
